examples.sentence: I fear the project deadline might be too tight for the team to meet without overtime.
examples.translation: I am worried that the project deadline may be too tight for the team to meet without working extra hours.

examples.sentence: She says she will join us, but I fear she might be stuck in traffic again.
examples.translation: She says she will come, but I am concerned she could be delayed by traffic again.

examples.sentence: We fear that the recent storms could cause significant damage to the crops this season.
examples.translation: We are worried that the recent storms might severely damage the crops this season.

examples.sentence: I fear I won't be able to attend the meeting due to a sudden appointment.
examples.translation: I'm afraid I cannot come to the meeting because of an unexpected appointment.

examples.sentence: I fear this report isn't as complete as it should be, so please review it carefully.
examples.translation: I'm afraid this report may be incomplete, so please check it thoroughly.

examples.sentence: I fear we may have underestimated the cost of the renovation project.
examples.translation: I'm afraid we might have miscalculated the renovation expenses.

examples.sentence: I fear that without your help, this venture is doomed to fail.
examples.translation: I strongly suspect that without your assistance, this project will fail.

examples.sentence: I fear no one can match her dedication and passion for the cause.
examples.translation: I strongly feel that no one equals her commitment and enthusiasm.

examples.sentence: I fear we've stumbled into yet another complicated situation with this deal.
examples.translation: I have a strong feeling that this deal has led us into another complex problem.

examples.sentence: If I'm honest, I didn't enjoy the movie as much as I expected.
examples.translation: Frankly, I did not like the movie as much as I thought I would.

examples.sentence: If I'm honest, I think the new policy might cause more problems than it solves.
examples.translation: To be frank, I believe the new policy could create additional issues.

examples.sentence: I have to say, if I'm honest, your presentation could use more data to support the argument.
examples.translation: Honestly, your presentation would benefit from including more supporting data.

examples.sentence: If I'm honest with you, I might reconsider my decision after I review the facts.
examples.translation: On the condition that I am honest with you, I may change my mind after checking the facts.

examples.sentence: You can trust me if I'm honest in my assessment of the situation.
examples.translation: You can rely on me provided that I give a truthful evaluation of the situation.

examples.sentence: If I'm honest about my feelings, then I must admit I was hurt by what happened.
examples.translation: If I truly express my feelings, I have to confess that I was hurt by the event.

examples.sentence: If I'm honest, the project will succeed only if everyone cooperates fully.
examples.translation: The project will succeed on the condition that I am honest and everyone fully collaborates.

examples.sentence: If I'm not there, start without me; the meeting room's booked at 3 PM sharp.
examples.translation: Begin without me if I haven't arrived; the meeting room is reserved at 3 PM exactly.

examples.sentence: Please begin the ceremony on time—if I'm not there, start without me.
examples.translation: If I am absent, please commence the ceremony promptly.

examples.sentence: If I'm not there when the game starts, just kick off without me; I'll join as soon as I arrive.
examples.translation: Start the game without me if I haven't arrived; I will join later.

examples.sentence: The guests will gather at 7, so if I'm not there, start without me to keep things moving.
examples.translation: Begin the event at 7 even if I have not arrived to keep the schedule.

examples.sentence: If I'm not there, start without me—honestly, I'd rather skip the whole affair.
examples.translation: If I don't attend, begin without me—as a matter of fact, I prefer not to participate.

examples.sentence: Tell them, if I'm not there, start without me; I have no interest in joining that boring discussion.
examples.translation: Inform them that if I am absent, begin without me; I don't want to take part in that dull talk.

examples.sentence: He joked, 'If I'm not there, start without me,' meaning he had no intention of showing up.
examples.translation: He humorously said that if he was absent, they should proceed without him, implying he wouldn't come.

examples.sentence: If it's all the same, I'd prefer to leave early to avoid the rush hour traffic.
examples.translation: If you don't mind, I would rather leave early to avoid heavy traffic.

examples.sentence: Would you, if it's all the same, mind switching seats with me so I can sit by the window?
examples.translation: If it makes no difference to you, could you swap seats so I can be next to the window?

examples.sentence: If it's all the same, let's stick with the original plan rather than making last-minute changes.
examples.translation: If you have no objection, let's keep the original plan instead of changing it at the last moment.

examples.sentence: Compare these two samples and see if it's all the same in texture and color.
examples.translation: Examine the two samples to determine whether they are identical in texture and color.

examples.sentence: If it's all the same to you, the fabric in this batch is exactly the same as the one we ordered last month.
examples.translation: If there is no difference to you, the fabric here matches that from our previous order.

examples.sentence: The conditions in both experiments were controlled so that, if it's all the same, the results can be compared fairly.
examples.translation: Both experiments had identical conditions to ensure the results are comparable.

examples.sentence: Check the documents carefully to confirm if it's all the same information or if there are discrepancies.
examples.translation: Review the documents thoroughly to see whether the information matches or differs.

examples.sentence: If it works, it works—no need to overcomplicate the system with unnecessary features.
examples.translation: If the system functions well, there is no need to add complicated features.

examples.sentence: Even though the design is simple, if it works, it works, and that's what matters most.
examples.translation: Although the design is basic, its functionality is what counts.

examples.sentence: The prototype may look rough, but if it works, it works; we can refine it later.
examples.translation: The initial model seems crude, but its functionality is what matters; improvements can come afterward.

examples.sentence: If the engine starts and runs smoothly, then if it works, it works, regardless of how it looks.
examples.translation: If the engine operates well, its appearance is secondary.

examples.sentence: Our makeshift shelter is a bit shabby, but if it works, it works—we just need to stay dry.
examples.translation: Our temporary shelter looks poor, but since it protects us, that's what counts.

examples.sentence: I know the shortcut is unconventional, but if it works, it works; we'll save time getting there.
examples.translation: The unusual shortcut may seem odd, but if it gets us there faster, it is acceptable.

examples.sentence: The old software has glitches, but if it works, it works, so we haven't upgraded yet.
examples.translation: Despite some bugs, the software performs adequately, so we haven't changed it.

examples.sentence: When I told him the truth, if looks could kill, he would have dropped dead on the spot.
examples.translation: When I revealed the truth, he gave me such an angry look it seemed deadly.

examples.sentence: Sarah gave me that look again—if looks could kill, I'd be a goner by now.
examples.translation: Sarah looked at me so angrily that it felt like she could kill me with her gaze.

examples.sentence: He glared at his opponent so fiercely that if looks could kill, the match would have ended immediately.
examples.translation: His hostile stare was so intense it seemed lethal.

examples.sentence: If looks could kill, the assassin's stare would have eliminated his target instantly.
examples.translation: If a deadly glare were real, the assassin's gaze would have instantly killed the victim.

examples.sentence: In a fantasy novel, if looks could kill, the dragon's glare would incinerate anything it falls upon.
examples.translation: In fiction, a deadly look from a dragon would burn whatever it looks at.

examples.sentence: If looks could kill, the statue's eyes would be the most dangerous weapon in the kingdom.
examples.translation: If a glare could cause death, the statue's eyes would be extremely lethal.

examples.sentence: Imagine a world where if looks could kill, people would have to wear sunglasses all the time.
examples.translation: In a hypothetical world where deadly looks exist, everyone would need sunglasses constantly.
